---
title: "orient_and_conform"
generated: true
description: "Orient an input tensor along a set of axes, and conform its overall dimension to equal that of a reference."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.numerics.tensor_ops`](../nitrix-numerics-tensor_ops.qmd)

*Re-exported here; defined in `nitrix._internal.util`.*

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:orient_and_conform -->
```python
orient_and_conform(input: Shaped[Array, ...], axis: Union[int, Sequence[int]], reference: Optional[Shaped[Array, ...]] = None, dim: Optional[int] = None) -> Shaped[Array, ...]
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:orient_and_conform -->
Orient an input tensor along a set of axes, and conform its overall
dimension to equal that of a reference.

::: {.callout-warning}
If both `reference` and `dim` are provided, then `dim` takes
precedence.
::
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:orient_and_conform -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `input` | `Shaped[Array, ...]` | — | — | Input tensor. |
| `axis` | `Union[int, Sequence[int]]` | — | — | Output axes along which the tensor's input dimensions should be reoriented. This should be an n-tuple, where n is the number of axes in the input tensor. If these axes are not in the same order in the input tensor, the input is transposed before being oriented. |
| `reference` | `Optional[Shaped[Array, ...]]` | — | `None` | Reference tensor. The output is unsqueezed so that its total dimension equals that of the reference. Either a reference or an explicit output dimension (`dim`) must be provided. |
| `dim` | `Optional[int]` | — | `None` | Number of tensor axes in the desired output. |

**Returns** `Shaped[Array, ...]`. Reoriented tensor with singleton axes appended to conform with the reference number of axes.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
